text.title: python-fastapi Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for production FastAPI.

rule.statement: Use @asynccontextmanager lifespan for startup/shutdown. Never use deprecated @app.on_event("startup").
rule.rationale: on_event is deprecated; lifespan handles both phases atomically.

rule.statement: Define separate Base, Create, Update, and Response schemas. Response uses ConfigDict(from_attributes=True). Never reuse one schema for all four roles.
rule.rationale: One-schema-fits-all leaks read-only fields into Create, allows clients to set id/created_at, and breaks API contracts.

rule.statement: Use Annotated type aliases (DBSession, CurrentUser) for dependencies. This eliminates repeated Depends() boilerplate.
rule.rationale: `db: Annotated[AsyncSession, Depends(get_db)]` reads cleanly; the alias factors away the noise.

rule.statement: Service functions are async, accept typed parameters, and return ORM model instances or None. Never return raw SQL results or dicts.
rule.rationale: Returning models lets the router serialize via response schema; dicts bypass type safety and the OpenAPI contract.

rule.statement: Use `await db.flush(); await db.refresh(obj)` to get generated IDs after insert without committing. Commit happens in get_db on success.
rule.rationale: flush+refresh exposes DB defaults to the service without releasing the transaction prematurely.

rule.statement: lifespan used, schemas separated, Annotated deps, async services return ORM, flush+refresh pattern.
Not an async REST API or team has no async experience and timeline is tight.
